original_abstract: Deep learning has revolutionized the fields of computer vision, natural language understanding, speech recognition, information retrieval, and more. However, with the progressive improvements in deep learning models, their number of parameters, latency, and resources required to train, among others, have all increased significantly. Consequently, it has become important to pay attention to these footprint metrics of a model as well, not just its quality. We present and motivate the problem of efficiency in deep learning, followed by a thorough survey of the five core areas of model efficiency (spanning modeling techniques, infrastructure, and hardware) and the seminal work there. We also present an experiment-based guide along with code for practitioners to optimize their model training and deployment. We believe this is the first comprehensive survey in the efficient deep learning space that covers the landscape of model efficiency from modeling techniques to hardware support. It is our hope that this survey would provide readers with the mental model and the necessary understanding of the field to apply generic efficiency techniques to immediately get significant improvements, and also equip them with ideas for further research and experimentation to achieve additional gains.
humanized_abstract: Deep learning has revolutionized many areas of the world such as computer vision, natural language understanding, speech recognition and information retrieval. While models are constantly improving, many trends such as numbers of parameters, latency and resources required to train the models are going in the wrong direction. It has become very important to also consider the footprint metrics of the models and balance them against their quality. This paper introduces the problem of efficiency in deep learning and conducts a survey of the efficiency research along five models of the relationships between modeling techniques, infrastructure and hardware as well as summarizes the notable contributions in each sub-field. This paper also provides an experimental guide and open-sourced code for model developers to train and deploy their models efficiently. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first of its kind to cover the model efficiency from modeling techniques to hardware support, which would help readers get an understanding of the high level structures of efficient deep learning and have the key knowledge points to apply very generic optimization techniques which are known to yield large improvements, as well as research and experiments that could provide incremental gains.
